scope: CIFAR-10 only — ResNet-20/32/44/56/110. ImageNet bottleneck blocks (ResNet-50/101/152) and 7x7 stem are out of scope.

framework.reasoning: PyTorch is the de-facto modern framework for vision research. The CIFAR-10 ResNets are small enough (≤1.7M params, 32x32 inputs) that they train on CPU in tolerable time and on a single consumer GPU in minutes-to-hours. CUDA is optional — code falls back to CPU. No HuggingFace integration needed; a single hand-written torchvision-style dataset suffices. Config is plain YAML loaded via PyYAML to keep the runtime dependency footprint minimal.

module_hierarchy.description: Defines the CIFAR-10 ResNet model (6n+2 layers) and the BasicBlock with Option-A identity shortcut.

public_methods.name: __init__
public_methods.signature: (in_planes: int, planes: int, stride: int = 1, shortcut_option: str = 'A')

public_methods.name: forward
public_methods.returns: Tensor of shape [B, planes, H', W']

public_methods.name: forward
public_methods.returns: Tensor with zero-padded extra channels and stride-2 downsampling (Option A).

public_methods.name: _init_weights
public_methods.returns: He init on conv weights; constant init on BN.

public_methods.name: build
public_methods.returns: ResNetCIFAR with n derived from name (resnet20→3, resnet32→5, resnet44→7, resnet56→9, resnet110→18).

module_hierarchy.description: CIFAR-10 dataset wrapper with paper-spec augmentation.

public_methods.name: __init__
public_methods.signature: (data_dir: str, batch_size: int = 128, num_workers: int = 2, val_size: int = 0)

public_methods.name: train_loader
public_methods.returns: DataLoader yielding (image, label) batches with paper augmentations applied.

public_methods.name: test_loader
public_methods.returns: DataLoader over 10k test images, no augmentation.

module_hierarchy.description: Paper-specified transforms: per-pixel mean subtraction, 4-pixel pad + random crop, random hflip.
SGD trainer with iteration-based LR schedule (drops at 32k and 48k) and optional warmup for ResNet-110.

public_methods.name: __init__
public_methods.signature: (model: nn.Module, train_loader, val_loader, test_loader, cfg: dict, device: torch.device)

public_methods.name: fit
public_methods.returns: Dict with per-iteration loss, per-epoch test accuracy, and best test accuracy.

module_hierarchy.description: Iteration-based step LR schedule + ResNet-110 warmup helper.

public_methods.name: __init__
public_methods.signature: (optimizer, base_lr: float, warmup_lr: float, warmup_iters: int, drop_iters: list[int], drop_factor: float = 10.0)

module_hierarchy.description: Deterministic seeding for torch / numpy / random.

tensor_flows.name: ResNetCIFAR_forward
steps: x = stage1: stack of n BasicBlocks (16 → 16, stride=1)  → [B, 16, 32, 32]
x = stage2: BasicBlock(16 → 32, stride=2) then (n-1) BasicBlocks (32 → 32, stride=1)  → [B, 32, 16, 16]
x = stage3: BasicBlock(32 → 64, stride=2) then (n-1) BasicBlocks (64 → 64, stride=1)  → [B, 64, 8, 8]

tensor_flows.name: BasicBlock_forward
steps: shortcut = x if (in_planes == planes and stride == 1) else IdentityPadShortcut(x)

tensor_flows.name: IdentityPadShortcut_forward (Option A)
steps: If stride>1: x = avg_pool2d(x, kernel_size=1, stride=stride) OR x = x[:, :, ::stride, ::stride]
If out_planes > in_planes: pad zero channels using F.pad(x, (0,0,0,0,0,out_planes-in_planes,0,0))

tensor_flows.name: training_iteration
steps: scheduler.step(iteration)  # adjusts LR based on current iteration

model.name: resnet20
model._shortcut_option_note: Option A = identity with zero-padded extra channels. Paper uses A for all CIFAR experiments (SIR conf 0.92).

training._weight_decay_note: Applied only to conv/FC weights, not BN parameters (SIR ambiguity, conf 0.70; widely used convention).

data._augment_spec: Per-pixel mean subtraction; 4-pixel zero-pad + random 32x32 crop; random horizontal flip.
data._mean_subtraction_note: ASSUMED: per-pixel rather than per-channel (SIR ambiguity, conf 0.75). Falls back to per-channel via config flag.

runtime.reason: Core deep learning framework; provides nn.Module, autograd, DataLoader, optim.
Mean image computation and array utilities.
Training progress bars in CLI and notebook.

entrypoints.purpose: Train a CIFAR-10 ResNet end-to-end and write checkpoints + final test error to disk.

cli_args.help: Where to write checkpoints and metrics.

entrypoints.purpose: Load a trained checkpoint and report test accuracy / error on CIFAR-10.

cli_args.help: Path to YAML config used at training time.

entrypoints.purpose: Run inference on a single image and print top-1 class.

risk_assessment.description: Per-pixel vs per-channel mean subtraction is ambiguous in the paper. Reproductions diverge here; per-channel is more common in modern code.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Expose `data.mean_subtraction` as a config flag; default to per-pixel to match paper wording, document per-channel as alternative.

risk_assessment.description: Whether weight decay applies to BN parameters is unstated. Modern PyTorch defaults include BN, which can hurt accuracy vs the paper.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Split optimizer param groups: conv/FC weights get weight_decay=1e-4; BN params and biases get weight_decay=0.

risk_assessment.description: ResNet-110 fails to converge without LR warmup. Easy to forget if user switches to resnet110 without enabling warmup.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Auto-enable warmup whenever model.name == 'resnet110' regardless of config flag.

risk_assessment.description: Iteration-based LR schedule with batch_size=128 makes the effective epoch count depend on dataset size (164 epochs if full 50k train, 182 if 45k/5k split).
risk_assessment.mitigation: Use the full 50k train split by default (val_size=0); LR schedule is iteration-based so total wall-clock is invariant.

risk_assessment.description: He initialization vs PyTorch default kaiming_normal_ differ in mode ('fan_in' vs 'fan_out') and gain. Original He et al. PReLU paper specifies fan_in with relu gain.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Explicitly call nn.init.kaiming_normal_(weight, mode='fan_in', nonlinearity='relu') in _init_weights.

risk_assessment.description: Stochastic results: ResNet-110 mean error has stddev ~0.16% across 5 runs. Single-seed reproductions may report values outside the paper's median.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Notebook trains ResNet-20 (lower variance, faster); README describes how to replicate the multi-seed protocol.
